Here's a paragraph about Hickory:

Hickory is a type of deciduous tree that belongs to the walnut family (Juglandaceae). The bark of the Hickory tree has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Hickory bark include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Hickory bark is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, inflammation, and digestive problems.
